featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 17 is Beanie for Brain Cancer Round, which makes the charity framing especially relevant but still does not force a bet.
The Panthers are the cleanest raw tip with Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Brian To'o all named after the Titans upset.
Roosters over Broncos and Dolphins over Warriors are strong reads, but both are away from pure banker territory.
Storm over Sea Eagles and Rabbitohs over Eels are the two tips most exposed to venue and momentum swings.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Do not force a Mark Hughes Foundation bet just because it is Beanie round.
The Broncos regain Reece Walsh and Payne Haas, so Roosters H2H may be shorter than the risk profile deserves.
The Warriors lose Jackson Ford long-term but still keep enough structure to trouble the Dolphins.
The Titans just beat Penrith, so Bulldogs over Titans is a confidence lean rather than a lock.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Wait for final 24-hour teams before any stake discussion.
Keep Round 17 price-watch candidates separate from actual charity bets.
Round 16 had no locked charity bet, so no ledger movement is recorded.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Panthers spine and middle leadership back at Townsville.
Dolphins full-strength outside backs and forward bench against a Warriors side missing Jackson Ford.
Roosters full spine against Brisbane's reshuffled halves.
Knights home spine of Kalyn Ponga, Fletcher Sharpe and Dylan Brown against a Tigers side still carrying injuries.

betCard.title: Round 17 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 25 June to Sunday 28 June 2026
why: The ledger is positive at +$0.53 and Beanie for Brain Cancer Round is a good week to stay disciplined.
Panthers H2H, Roosters H2H and Dolphins H2H are the only price-watch candidates from the current read.
None becomes a charity bet without a fresh TAB price, stake, match, and explicit user confirmation.
risks: Beanie round theme can create emotional pressure to force a bet.
Several favourites have short-price risk despite strong team-list reads.
Any new charity play needs recorded odds, stake, match, and final team context before kickoff.

watchlist: Price-watch Panthers over Cowboys only if the Townsville premium is enough to keep the price useful.
Price-watch Roosters over Broncos, but respect Walsh and Haas returning at Suncorp.
Price-watch Dolphins over Warriors, but do not overreact to Jackson Ford's injury without a market edge.
Do not bet Rabbitohs v Eels, Bulldogs v Titans, Sea Eagles v Storm, or Knights v Tigers without a clean late-mail edge.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 17 is Beanie round, but the ledger still starts with discipline
lines: As of Wednesday, 24 June 2026, Round 17 team lists and late mail have been checked against current published reports.
Round 17 has eight games: Eels v Rabbitohs, Titans v Bulldogs, Broncos v Roosters, Dolphins v Warriors, Cowboys v Panthers, Sea Eagles v Storm, Raiders v Dragons, and Knights v Wests Tigers.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 16 charity bet was locked.
Round 17 is live as a preview board only; no new charity bet is locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Beanie round is exactly when the bet discipline matters
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger is 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
Panthers over Cowboys is the cleanest raw tip, with the Penrith first-choice spine and senior middle named.
Roosters over Broncos and Dolphins over Warriors are also price-watch candidates, but both have opponent-backbone risk.
No Round 17 charity bet should be recorded without a fresh TAB price, stake and user confirmation.

sections.title: Round 17 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 17 fixture board
lines: Eels v Rabbitohs - Thursday 25 June, 7:50pm at CommBank Stadium.
Titans v Bulldogs - Friday 26 June, 6:00pm at Cbus Super Stadium.
Broncos v Roosters - Friday 26 June, 8:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Dolphins v Warriors - Saturday 27 June, 3:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Cowboys v Panthers - Saturday 27 June, 5:30pm at Queensland Country Bank Stadium.
Sea Eagles v Storm - Saturday 27 June, 7:35pm at 4 Pines Park.
Raiders v Dragons - Sunday 28 June, 2:00pm at GIO Stadium.
Knights v Wests Tigers - Sunday 28 June, 4:05pm at McDonald Jones Stadium.
Sharks have the bye.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who gets upgraded or held back this week
lines: Upgrade: Panthers full-strength response, Roosters spine, Dolphins reinforcements, Raiders at GIO, and Knights home spine.
Hold: Bulldogs because the Titans just beat Penrith and play at Cbus.
Downgrade: Warriors because Jackson Ford joins Roger Tuivasa-Sheck on the long-term injury list.
Volatility bucket: Eels v Rabbitohs, Sea Eagles v Storm, Titans v Bulldogs, and Knights v Wests Tigers.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 17 team-list details driving the card
lines: Jackson Ford is out 10-12 weeks with a pectoral injury, weakening a Warriors side already without Roger Tuivasa-Sheck.
Penrith name Dylan Edwards, Brian To'o, Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Liam Martin for the Townsville trip.
The Broncos name Reece Walsh, Kotoni Staggs and Payne Haas, but Ezra Mam is out and Thomas Duffy partners Ben Hunt.
The Dolphins regain Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Selwyn Cobbo, Thomas Flegler, Max Plath and Kulikefu Finefeuiaki from the Round 16 rest cycle.
The Tigers name Adam Doueihi and Alex Twal back, but Jock Madden and Patrick Herbert are out after Round 16 injuries.
